manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: note, bell, hawk, antarctica, shadow, dragon, bank, ship, phoenix.
Your opponent's team words are: file, park, straw, tie, film, england, horn, mexico.
Distractor words are: whip, model, brush, bar, swing, capital, day.
The kill words are: club.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

content.thinking:
I have two remaining words: hawk, shadow.

Let me think about what could connect them:
- hawk: a bird of prey, could relate to hunting, flying, sharp vision
- shadow: darkness, silhouette, following, darkness

What could connect both?
- "dark" - hawks can be dark colored, shadow is dark
- "follow" - hawks follow prey, shadow follows you
- "silhouette" - both can be seen as silhouettes
- "prey" - hawks hunt prey, shadow could be associated with stalking
- "raptor" - hawk is a raptor, but shadow doesn't relate
- "hunt" - hawks hunt, but shadow doesn't really hunt
- "tail" - hawks have tails, you can "tail" someone (shadow them)

Actually, "tail" could work! Hawk has a tail (physical), and to "shadow" someone means to follow/tail them.

Let me check the other words to make sure "tail" doesn't accidentally point to them:
- file: no strong connection
- park: no
- straw: no
- tie: no
- film: no
- england: no
- mexico: no
- whip: no
- model: no
- brush: has bristles like a tail? weak
- bar: no
- swing: no
- capital: no
- day: no
- club: no

"tail" seems safe and connects to both hawk and shadow reasonably well.

Another option: "dark" - hawks can be dark, shadow is associated with darkness
- But "dark" might be too general and could accidentally connect to "film" (dark films)

Another option: "follow" - hawks follow prey, shadow follows you
- This is good but might be less clear for hawk

I think "tail" is a good choice.
